title: Run-level A/B coincidence-rate model versus B covariance

primary_metric.name: held-out run A-any given B-any coincidence-rate RMSE

finding: Run-level A/B coincidence rate is mainly a sparse acceptance/current-target observable and does not explain the large B2-local residual covariance.

leakage.interpretation: each prediction holds out one complete run
rate-model features exclude run id, event id, A-stack counts, and label columns
ExtraTrees trained on shuffled run-rate targets should not match the nominal held-out model
a near-zero held-out run-level rate error would imply leakage or a deterministic target
